tickets.summary: [JSONPlaceholder] No authentication on any endpoint — 20 BOLA/BFLA/auth-bypass observations
tickets.description:
## Summary
All 41 JSONPlaceholder endpoints accept unauthenticated requests. 20 security test scenarios confirmed that GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE operations succeed without any credentials across all 6 resources (posts, comments, albums, photos, todos, users).

## Root Cause
By-design: JSONPlaceholder is a public mock API with no authentication layer. Contract model declares `auth: none` for all endpoints.

## OWASP Categories
- API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization
- API2:2023 Broken Authentication
- API5:2023 Broken Function Level Authorization
- API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows

## Evidence
- 20 anomalies across SCN-001 through SCN-044
- All returned HTTP 200/201 where 401/403 was probed
- Covers CRUD operations on all resource types

## Recommendation
No action for mock API. Document as baseline security posture. If adapting this API pattern for production, implement OAuth 2.0/JWT auth, ownership checks, and RBAC.

tickets.summary: [JSONPlaceholder] User PII and resource data exposed without access control — 5 data exposure findings
tickets.description:
## Summary
5 anomalies confirm that user PII (name, email, phone, address) and resource data (posts, todos) are accessible to any caller without access control. Nested routes enable cross-user data enumeration.

## Root Cause
By-design: The API serves static synthetic test data without access control or field-level filtering.

## OWASP Categories
- API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization
- API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization

## Evidence
- GET /users/1 returns full PII object (email, phone, address, company)
- GET /users/1/posts and /users/1/todos expose cross-user data
- POST /comments accepts spoofed email fields

## Recommendation
No action for mock API (synthetic data). For production: implement field-level ACL, redact sensitive fields, use cursor-based pagination.

tickets.summary: [JSONPlaceholder] No input validation — mass assignment, XSS, and SSRF payloads accepted verbatim
tickets.description:
## Summary
8 anomalies confirm the API accepts all input without validation: mass assignment of userId/postId fields, XSS script tags stored in post titles, and SSRF payloads (internal IPs) stored in photo URL fields.

## Root Cause
By-design: JSONPlaceholder echoes submitted data back without schema validation, sanitization, or server-side processing. SSRF payloads are stored but never fetched.

## OWASP Categories
- API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization (mass assignment)
- API7:2023 Server Side Request Forgery (stored URL payloads)
- API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration (XSS)

## Evidence
- POST /posts with arbitrary userId → 201 (mass assignment)
- POST /posts with `<script>alert(1)</script>` in title → 201 (XSS stored)
- POST /photos with `http://169.254.169.254` in url → 201 (SSRF payload)
- PUT and PATCH variants also accept payloads

## Recommendation
No action for mock API. For production: allowlist input validation, strip unknown fields, sanitize HTML, validate URLs against domain allowlist.

tickets.summary: [JSONPlaceholder] Rate limiting present but generous (1000/window) — 3 false-positive anomalies
tickets.description:
## Summary
3 anomalies flagged rate limiting as not enforced, but response headers reveal `x-ratelimit-limit: 1000` with `x-ratelimit-remaining: 999`. Rate limiting IS active but the threshold was not reached during testing.

## Root Cause
False positive in anomaly detection. Rate limit headers prove enforcement exists. The 1000/window threshold is intentionally generous for a public test API.

## Evidence
- Response headers: `x-ratelimit-limit: 1000`, `x-ratelimit-remaining: 999`
- Cloudflare CDN (cf-cache-status: HIT) provides additional infrastructure protection
- Test suite sent ~63 requests, far below the 1000 threshold

## Recommendation
Reclassify as false positive. No action needed.

tickets.summary: [JSONPlaceholder] HSTS and X-Frame-Options security headers missing
tickets.description:
## Summary
Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) and X-Frame-Options headers are absent. The API does serve over HTTPS and includes x-content-type-options: nosniff.

## Root Cause
Infrastructure configuration: Heroku does not add HSTS by default. Express app does not use helmet middleware.

## Evidence
- Response headers from SCN-025 show no Strict-Transport-Security or X-Frame-Options
- HTTPS is active, x-content-type-options: nosniff is present
- Infrastructure: Heroku + Cloudflare + Express

## Recommendation
Low priority. Enable HSTS in Cloudflare dashboard. Add helmet middleware to Express. These are defense-in-depth headers with minimal impact on a JSON API.
